calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Proof Of Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-16-2011 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for and with Takiyah Smith. Carloyn Espey for Lavani Wright.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. The accounting petitions were taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Brown.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2011 Text Ruling

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-14-2011 Continued To Apr-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for Robert Bockelman (former attorney for Takiyah Smith). Carolyn Espey for Lavani Wright. Greer Smith for Takiyah Smith. Robert Ferris (former attorney for Takiyah Smith).

The Court stated that it wanted to know how the U.S. Treasury bonds managed to be deposited and negotiated and that Ms. Espey could respond but that it wants her response in writing as well. Ms. Espey responded stating that the bonds were taken to the bank, deposted, that a check was written, and then cashed to get the proceeds. In addition, she stated that some of the money was used to pay funeral bills, other related bills, and that the rest was distributed to all five of the siblings, that the checkbook was at the house, that her client never took the checkbook, and that she mistakenly stated that only her client had access to the checkbook. Ms. Smith stated that Ms. Espey's client filed what she chose to file, that she didn't file all of the checks, that when she asked for them that Ms. Espey's client sent them, and then made accusations. The Court stated that it wants a declaration from Lavani regarding the treasury bonds and the $5,700.00. In addition, the Court noted that it had stated at a separate hearing that Mr. Bockelman's fees could be filed in the petitions for final distribution instead of his own fee request and that it wanted to hear arguments on the fee request so that Mr. Ferris doesn't have to come back. Mr. Ferris stated that they submitted the amount for the fees, that there is an agreement as to the statutory fees, that they requested a small amount of extraordinary fees, that the prep for the conservatorship was done soley by them, and that the costs for the Special Needs Trust were to be paid from the Probate Estate. Ms. Espey stated that her only comments in her pleadings stated that anything related to the conservatorship should be approved in the conservatorship prior to payment. Ms. Smith stated that there was an agreement in writing that their fees would be split 50/50; 25% each since they were splitting half. At the conclusion of argument, the Court continued both petitions for final distribution to April 4, 2011, ordered Lavani's declaration to be on file by the end of business on March 29, 2011, and stated that it would take the petitions under submission on April 4, 2011. In response to Ms. Smith the Court confirmed that the parties would have an opportunity to argue. Mr. Ferris' appearance is excused at the next hearing.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for and with Takiyah Smith. Carolyn Espey for Lavani Wright. Robert Ferris for Former Attorney for Robert Bockelman.

The Court stated that it would like a response from Takiyah in reference to the allegations in Lavani's petition regarding the Wells Fargo account. Ms. Espey and Ms. Greer are okay with the Court considering Mr. Ferris' fee request. The matters were continued to March 7, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The response is due by March 1, 2011. The Court waives Takiyah's appearance at the next hearing.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Getz.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of The House And On Filing Of The Petition For Final Distributiion
Off Calendar Apr-12-2010 Continued To Jun-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Co-Administrator To Comply With November 16, 2006 Stipulation
Off Calendar Feb-01-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for and with Takiyah Smith. Carolyn Espey for and with Lavani Wright.

Counsel reported that the listing agents did a walk through of the property, that they gave an estimated listing price and a list of suggestions for improvements that needed to made to the property. In addition, they stated that Takiyah vacated the premises, that Takiyah left some of her personal property in the house, that there were some items that belonged to the Father and the Mother that were also left on the premises, and that some of the Decedent's personal property was missing/taken. Furthermore, Counsel presented oral argument with regard to reimbursements not being paid, untimely responses, Takiyah getting more than 1/5 share, and how the estate personal property is to be stored/maintained. The Petition to Compel Co-Administrator to Comply with November 16, 2006 Stipulation filed on August 11, 2009 is moot and will be taken off calendar. The Court stated that all of the personal property is to be moved out of the premises by February 15, 2010 in order for the house to be put on the market, that the listing agreement can be signed by February 22, 2010 and that the month of March can be used to do upgrades. Anything that is left on the premises after February 15th is to be disposed of. If Takiyah's property has to be removed, she is to bear the costs. Status on sale of the house and on filing of the petition for final distribution was set for April 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Reports by both Counsel regarding status on where things stand are due by close of business Tuesday April 6, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.
Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Compel Co-Administrator To Comply With November 16, 2006 Stipulation
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Continued To Feb-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for Takiyah Smith. Carolyn Espy for Lavani Wright.

Counsel stated that they agreed to have Michael Freethby and Jonathan Crossley from Prudential Realty list and sell the property and that Ms. Wright would sign the listing agreement immediately. The Court was pleased to see that Takiyah Smith and Malik had vacated the property and that the bank account issue had been resolved. The matter was continued to February 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the sale of the property. Takiyah Smith's powers were reinstated. Counsel Greer Smith will prepare and submit the order. The Court directed Counsel to figure out the issue regarding the missing property.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Off Calendar

Petition To Compel Co-Administrator To Comply With November 16, 2006 Stipulation
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Continued To Dec-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for and with Takiah Smith. Carolyn Espy for and with Lavani Wright.

Ms. Greer Smith stated that the only impediment to moving forward was that the powers were going to be suspeneded, that she brought a stipulation that would work everything out and that the property taxes were an issue. In addition, Ms. Greer Smith stated that her Client had a place for her and her Daughter but that she needed a family member to take Malik. Furthermore, she stated that if no one was willing to take Malik, that the offered move out date was January 7th. Ms. Espy stated that the property taxes had been paid, that they were not told that the bank account had been closed, that she felt Takiah was not handling her fiduciary responsibilties and requested she be removed. In addition, Ms. Espy raised the issue regarding some checks that had been written on the Wells Fargo account that had been signed by the Decedent and questioned what they were for. The Court suspended Takiah Smith's powers and will issue a citation for her to appear on December 14, 2009 and show cause why she should not be removed. By December 9, 2009 Takiah Smith needs to show and prove that She and Malik have vacated the property, file an Inventory and Appraisal showing the amount of cash in the account with proof, with a bank letter and cooperate with Lavani to prepare the property for sale. The matter was continued to December 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Compel Co-Administrator To Comply With November 16, 2006 Stipulation
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Continued To Nov-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Espy for and with Lavani Wright (Co-Administrator). Greer Smith for and with Takiah Smith. (Co-Administrator/Respondent).

The Court stated that the order granting a special needs for Malik Smith on case number 290699 was signed on September 21, 2009 and that status on filing of the $91,000.00 bond in the special needs trust new case number and filing of the sepcial needs trust matter had not been set. Therefore, status on the above was set for November 2, 2009. The Court informed all Parties that an estate account needed to be opened, that the property in which Takiah and Malik resided needed to be sold pursuant to the November 16, 2006 agreement and that the estate needed to be distributed. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation which is to be completed no later than November 6, 2009. Status of mediation is set for November 16, 2009. Order for the same is to be signed after the hearing. The Court also stated that if the matter does not get resolved, that it would consider suspension of somebody's powers because the joint efforts of the Co-Administrators was not successful and that the property needed to be vacated. All three Counsel are to select a real estate Agent for the mediator to choose from. The property is not to be self sold. Ms. Boessenecker is to be part of the mediation. Ms. Espy will notify Ms. Boessenecker in writing of the mediation. Ms. Espy will also notify the Court by November 10, 2009 as to the outcome of mediation. All communication between all Counsel is to be done in writing. The Parties agreed to Lavani Wright opening a checking account today and Takiah Smith to complete it on Friday. Niether Co-Adminisrator is to write any checks on the new account.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Family Allowance
Off Calendar Sep-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Malik Smith. Carolyn Espey for Lavani Wright (Co-Administrator). Greer Smith for and with Takiyah Smith (Petitioner).

Ms. Greer stated that the allowance was mandatory due to Malik being a disabled adult, that he was the only person entitled to a family allowance and that Malik was not his Client's dependent. In addition, Ms. Greer provided a summary of costs and expenses needed to provide care for Malik and stated her Client and Malik could not move out without the family allowance. Ms. Espey stated that the petition to compel an accounting was set for october 19, 2009, that the Petitioner would not stick to the stipulation and that her Client would request someone else be appointed. Ms. Boessenceker stated that it was a difficult situation, that the money was needed for them to move out, that it could be done as a preliminary disrtibution not a family allowance, suggested the Public Administrator be appointed and stated that someone needed to make sure the bank account does not get escheated to the State. The Court stated that it was leaning towards denying the petition, authorizing $2,000.00 be paid to the movers with money coming from Malik's share and that the Public Administrator issue would be addressed on October 19, 2009. Ms. Espey stated that her Client was agreeable with a preliminary distribution to her Brother and Sister. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Family Allowance
Off Calendar Sep-08-2009 Continued To Sep-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Family Allowance
Off Calendar Aug-17-2009 Continued To Sep-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Family Allowance
Off Calendar Jul-20-2009 Continued To Aug-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for and with Takiyah Smith (Co-Administrator and Conservator of Maleek Smith). Carolyn Espey for and with Lavani Wright (Co-Administrator).

Ms. Greer suggested the matter be put over to be heard with the Special Needs Trust on August 17, 2009. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Takiyah Smith residing in the property, the parties not being able to agree on a broker, establishing a joint account, the sale of the property, the family allowance and the assistance needed to provide care for Maleek. The Court stated that it would not approve the family allowance and that the housing situation needed to be arranged. The matter was continued to August 17, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition For Family Allowance
Off Calendar Jun-22-2009 Continued To Jul-20-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Gary Lieberman appearing for petitioners Talim Wright and Lavani Wright. Robert Bockelman appearing for and with Takiyan Smith. Two competing petitions. One brother is disabled. The issue of a conservatorship or Special Needs Trust discussed. Court wants counsel to meet and confer to see if they can work something out.
At 10:20am, Counsel and Ms. Wright returned to court with an agreement and stipulation. Takiyan Smith and Lavani Wright will serve as co-administrator with full independent powers. Bond is waived. The residence at 821 Capitol Avenue should not be sold for 18 months. Malik Smith and Takiyan Smith will reside in the residence without rent. Takiyan will petition for appointment as conservator for Malik. Fees and costs will be an expense of the estate. Takiyan agreed to have visit of the house with reasonable notice. Expenses of 821 Capitol Avenue will be expenses of the estate. Counsel will prepare a written agreement and an order. Judge: Raymond J. Arata. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-18-2006 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-30-2006 Continued To Oct-10-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Gary Lieberman appearing for Talim Wright and Lavani Wright. Lavani Wright is in court. Robert Bockelman appearing for and with objector Takiyah Smith. Mr. Lieberman proposes to have co-administrator appointed. Mr. Bockelman has worked with the decedent on her estate plan and he knows her wishes. He is asking the court to honor her estate plan. Mr. Lieberman objects to the Court honoring unsign estate plan. Mr. Bockelman will a petition within next week. Matter is continued to 10/10. Mr. Bockelman's petition can be set on the same date. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Teena Paxton CSR#1558. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
